svg.title: GPT-Voice GitHub Pages landing page desktop design
svg.desc: Editable 1440-pixel desktop design source showing the complete localized one-page layout, language selector, approved copy hierarchy, shadcn component placements, fresh application screenshots, progressive video area, provider signal-flow infographic, twelve-item functionality FAQ, and calls to action.

text.tspan: Writing clear, well-structured prompts takes time. GPT-Voice turns
speech into prompt-ready text, then helps you translate it and remove
grammar errors, repetition, and filler—with less effort.

g.text: Transcription, retry, translation, prettification, and provider choice—shown in the real app.
60 seconds · 60 fps · Voice-over and sound · Optional closed captions · Transcript

text.tspan: Retry only after a voice provider error.

g.text: Copied to clipboard · saved in local history
Only if the voice provider returns an error
Fix grammar, repetition & filler · preserve meaning

text.tspan: *Subject to ChatGPT plan, availability, fair-use, and provider limits.
More providers may follow where technically and legally viable.
No compatibility or timing is promised.

g.desc: Obsolete tabbed provider-state reference retained only as hidden source history; it is not part of the approved design.

text.tspan: Bring an API key with available billing

g.text: Availability, billing, and quotas follow the OpenAI account.
Independent project · Not affiliated with or endorsed by OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google

g.desc: Non-rendered editor history for the deleted feature-inventory section; this group is not an implementation or public-asset source.
g.text: The controls stay close. Your prompts stay in flow.
Speak, translate, clean, and keep moving—without breaking your flow.
Record · stop · cancel · translate · prettify · retry
Copy again or clear local history.
Ollama or vLLM · prompt and generation controls
Voice or selected text returns ready to paste.
Copy, page, or clear successful results.
Native feedback from recording to error.
Windows and Linux packages; four app languages.
Settings, sessions, encrypted secrets, and history stay in the user-data directory; transmitted content follows the chosen service.

text.tspan: Prettify, history, shortcuts, and model control.

g.text: How do I record and transcribe speech?
Which transcription providers can I use?
Can I retry without recording again?
Which Ollama and vLLM models can Prettify use?
Can I pause, resume, or cancel an action?
How does Ollama VRAM loading and unloading work?
Which app languages, operating systems, and package formats are supported?
Write better prompts faster, with less effort.
Speak naturally, translate for the model or task, and clean rough language without opening more tools.
Source available under the PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 license.
Desktop voice-to-text for better AI prompts, faster.
Independent · Not affiliated with OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google
